opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I'm covered head-to-toe in these huge, itchy welts that keep moving around my body. My skin feels like it's burning and I'm honestly terrified my throat is going to close up. It came on out of nowhere a few hours ago and it's spreading fast.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started about six hours ago and it's getting worse and spreading really quickly — I got scared it might be a serious allergic reaction.

symptom_narrative.onset: about six hours ago — came on suddenly, first on my belly
symptom_narrative.character: big raised itchy welts, some small bumps and some huge patches joined together, skin feels like it's burning
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch is unbearable, honestly the worst itch I've ever had
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: started on my tummy, now it's all over my trunk, arms and legs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the welts come up in one spot then fade after a couple of hours and new ones pop up somewhere else — worse at night and when I scratch
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: scratching makes it flare up more, and it seems worse in the warmth
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: just the itching and burning skin — I keep worrying about my throat but it feels okay so far
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had anything like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't sit still or think about anything else, I'm really agitated

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a new brand of ibuprofen I took yesterday for a bit of pain
current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: took some about 12 hours before the rash started

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: my partner is here with me and getting anxious too

communication_style.typical_message_style: writes long anxious messages, keeps returning to the fear of her throat closing

objective_data_offered.value: widespread raised itchy welts over the trunk, arms and legs — pale in the middle with a red edge, some tiny and some large joined-up patches; individual welts fade within a couple of hours and new ones appear elsewhere
no visible swelling of the lips, tongue, face or eyelids; no blisters, no scaling, no broken skin
speaking in full sentences comfortably, no noisy breathing heard on the call
partner says she is very distressed and keeps scratching and rubbing her arms and legs, but is not short of breath and her voice sounds normal
